pi_items.source: farmOS official documentation at https://farmos.org/guide/logs/ confirms Observation as one of four default log types (activity, harvest, input, observation)
Valid silo identifier; consistent with OSHA confined space entry requirements which mandate specific location identification
OSHA and silo safety guidelines at https://www.tdi.texas.gov/tips/safety/silos.html and https://www.fbn.com/community/blog/silo-safety confirm ventilation is mandatory before silo entry
Standard safety protocol language for atmosphere testing results; confirmed in OSHA 1910.146 Appendix B on atmospheric testing procedures
2 ppm is below occupational exposure limits for NO2 (ACGIH TWA: 3 ppm, STEL: 5 ppm; NIOSH PEL: 5 ppm) indicating safe atmosphere per https://extension.psu.edu/silo-gases-the-hidden-danger
ppm (parts per million) is the standard unit for measuring toxic gas concentrations in confined space atmosphere testing, as confirmed in OSHA standards

NO2 (nitrogen dioxide) is the primary toxic gas of concern in grain silo safety, reaching concentrations of 200-4000 ppm within 2 days after filling per https://extension.psu.edu/silo-gases-the-hidden-danger

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a farmOS Observation log entry for a silo gas safety check. All metadata claims are verified: Observation is a documented default log type in farmOS. The required notes contain standard silo safety terminology (Silo #2, Ventilation, safe for entry) consistent with OSHA confined space entry protocols. The quantity measurement of 2 ppm NO2 is verified as a safe atmosphere level (below ACGIH 3 ppm TWA and NIOSH 5 ppm PEL limits), recorded in the standard unit (ppm) used for gas monitoring in silo atmosphere testing. This task simulates a legitimate pre-entry safety check workflow for grain silos.
